danger_signals.visual_form: Minecrawler radar system activates during atmospheric entry
danger_signals.meaning: Imminent threat; anti-air missiles will launch if player enters radar zone

danger_signals.visual_form: Array of varied enemies with distinct visual designs per level
danger_signals.meaning: Combat encounter required; different enemy types signal different threat levels

danger_signals.visual_form: Control center architecture and pilot presence
danger_signals.meaning: Final confrontation danger; requires specific strategy to eliminate pilot

danger_signals.visual_form: Descending through environment without ribbon chute deployed
danger_signals.meaning: Environmental hazard; health loss imminent unless parachute activated

danger_signals.visual_form: Containment barriers of energy stream during extraction sequence
danger_signals.meaning: Boundary hazard; touching walls causes health loss and deceleration
Progression blocked; requires 'World's Smallest Nuclear Explosion' weapon

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible item appearing in energy stream post-level completion
safety_signals.meaning: Recovery opportunity; grants 150% health for next level start

safety_signals.visual_form: Integrated parachute system on Kurt's outfit
safety_signals.meaning: Safe descent enabled; can survive long falls and utilize updrafts indefinitely

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectable item enabling smart bomb feature
safety_signals.meaning: Tactical advantage unlocked; Max bomber support available for area targets

safety_signals.visual_form: Minecrawler disintegration animation and energy stream emergence
safety_signals.meaning: Safe progression; escape sequence initiated with extraction opportunity

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Jim Dandy space station (orbital base of operations)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Orbit around Earth; designated safe extraction point

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Each Minecrawler has unique aesthetic, enemy design, and control center layout per level
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Harvesting Earth's natural resources; crushing cities and people
Natural resources being extracted; destroyed cities and civilian casualties visible

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Minecrawlers active, harvesting resources, destroying terrain and structures
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies patrolling and attacking; environmental destruction ongoing; radar systems active

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enemy array positioned throughout level with distinct visual designs
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies engage player; varied attack patterns based on level design and enemy type

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Control center architecture with pilot visible; unique aesthetic per Minecrawler
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Pilot employs level-specific combat strategy; elimination required for progression

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Minecrawler disintegration animation; structure collapses into energy stream
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Extraction sequence triggered; Kurt pursued by energy stream suction; health power-up chase phase

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Kurt descending from orbit to Minecrawler surface; radar system visible and active
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Radar triggers missile launches; player must dodge anti-air projectiles during descent

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Bombing ship, glider vehicle, snowboarding terrain variants
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Mission-specific gameplay modes activated; varied control mechanics per level type

environmental_storytelling.example: Every level features completely different aesthetic, enemy types, and control center layout—visual design communicates distinct invasion vessel identity and strategy

environmental_storytelling.visual_form: Strip-mining machinery and harvested terrain visible throughout Minecrawlers
environmental_storytelling.example: Visual evidence of natural resource depletion communicates invasion objective and environmental destruction
City-sized Minecrawler vehicles crushing people and cities; scale establishes existential threat narrative
Jim Dandy space station in safe orbit versus hostile Minecrawler surface; visual separation establishes sanctuary and danger zones
Post-level extraction through energy stream with health hazards communicates dimension-bridging alien technology and escape urgency

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Scans for intruders; triggers anti-air missile launch if player touches radar zone
Patrolling, engaging, attacking with varied strategies per level design
Employs unique combat strategy specific to individual Minecrawler; must be eliminated for progression
Flies bomber over designated target area and drops ordnance when called via sniper mode
Deploys ribbon chute to survive atmospheric entry and dodge radar-triggered missiles
Disintegrates and is sucked back into energy stream; initiates extraction sequence
Pulls defeated Minecrawler through dimensional rift; creates health hazard zones and deceleration effects on player contact

notes: MDK communicates world-state primarily through environmental manifestation of alien invasion mechanics. Danger signals are tied to active threat systems (radar, enemies, environmental hazards). Safety signals emerge from player equipment (ribbon chute) and completion rewards (health pickups). Faction distinction is visual and structural rather than color/symbol-based: alien Minecrawlers are identified by their massive scale, unique per-level aesthetics, and harvesting mechanics; human forces are represented by the orbital Jim Dandy sanctuary. Political situation is communicated implicitly through the strip-mining invasion narrative and civilian destruction. No explicit faction colors or symbols documented; identity is conveyed through architecture, scale, and behavioral patterns.
